Author: Sandall et al.
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Preterm birth, mode of birth, maternal satisfaction
Key_Result: Preterm birth RR 0.76 (CI 0.64 to 0.91). Higher spontaneous vaginal birth rate. Higher maternal satisfaction. No difference in CS rate or neonatal mortality.
Notes: Updated from 2016 review. 15 RCTs included. Most robust evidence base.

Author: Allen et al.
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Mode of birth, analgesia, intervention rate
Key_Result: Reduced epidural use, reduced augmentation, reduced instrumental birth. No harm for any subgroup including moderate-risk.
Notes: Included moderate-risk women, broadening applicability of findings.

Author: Tracy et al.
Key_Result: Caseload model broadly cost-neutral or modestly less costly than standard care. Reduced intervention rates.

Author: Forster et al.
Key_Result: Similar outcomes to caseload model for normal birth rate, higher than standard care.
Notes: Team model more compatible with rostered working, partial continuity.

Author: NHS England Care Quality Commission
Key_Result: Women who knew their midwife during labour reported significantly higher satisfaction. Named midwife model unevenly implemented.
Notes: Survey data, not RCT. Implementation barriers noted.

Author: Homer et al.
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Maternal satisfaction, mode of birth, neonatal outcomes
Key_Result: High satisfaction. Normal birth rates significantly higher than hospital-based standard care for equivalent risk groups.
Notes: NZ LMC model closest international comparator to proposed Galway model.

Author: Walsh and Devane
Key_Result: Consistent direction of effect favoring midwife-led care. Insufficient Irish-specific RCT evidence at that time.
Notes: Irish context underpowered at time of publication. Cochrane 2024 update more applicable.

Author: RCOG and RCM
Key_Result: Identifies workforce flexibility, caseload size of 35 to 40 per WTE midwife, and out-of-hours compensation as essential enablers.
Notes: Policy and guidance, not primary research. Useful for implementation planning.

Author: Department of Health Ireland
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Service organisation and continuity as objective
Key_Result: Named midwife by 2020 as target. Target not met by 2025. Implementation variable across HSE regions.
Notes: Grey literature. Policy intent vs implementation gap is central to this review.

Author: Renfrew et al.
Key_Result: Full package of midwifery care could prevent 83 percent of maternal deaths, stillbirths, and neonatal deaths globally. Continuity central to full package.
Notes: Broader than continuity of carer specifically but provides global framing.

Author: Massachusetts Birth Centre Collaborative
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Transfer rate, mode of birth, neonatal outcomes
Key_Result: Transfer rate to hospital 12.4 percent, within international benchmarks. CS rate 5.2 percent for centre births. No increase in adverse neonatal outcomes.
Notes: Directly relevant to proposed Galway model. Transfer protocol design informed by this.

Author: Boston Midwifery Research Exchange
Primary_Outcome_Measure: Family experience and transition to parenthood
Key_Result: Strong qualitative evidence for relational benefit of known midwife at birth. Quantitative outcomes consistent with wider literature.
Notes: Exchange cohort. Author participated in data gathering phase, September 2025.
